member.name: Dr Scott Arthur
member.synopsis: Dr Scott Arthur is the Labour MP for Edinburgh South West , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the potential risks of age verification methods involving facial or biometric data to enforce a social media ban for under-16s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have asked Ofcom to rapidly assess what constitutes highly effective age assurance for establishing whether someone is over 16, prioritising data privacy and security, and considering how it can work for all users.The ICO’s age-appropriate design code a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Musculoskeletal Disorders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to increase awareness of Inclusion Body Myositis and other rare progressive muscle disorders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains committed to improving outcomes for people living with rare diseases, including inclusion body myositis and other rare progressive muscle disorders, through the UK Rare Diseases Framework and successive England Rare Diseases Action ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Prosecutions: Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when he plans to launch a consultation on proposed regulations relating to costs orders under Section 17 of the Prosecution of Offences Act 1985.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Victims and Court Act 2026 introduces an enabling power for the Lord Chancellor to set the rates at which private prosecutors can recover costs from central funds under section 17 of the Prosecution of Offences Act 1985. The Government will consult in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Musculoskeletal Disorders: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to incentivise research into Inclusion Body Myositis and other rare progressive muscle disorders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). The NIHR funds clinical, public health, and social care research and works in partnership with the National Health Service, universities, local government, o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Delivery Services: Robots
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment the Department has made of the level of need for further regulation of autonomous delivery robots operating on public pavements and highways; and whether the Department plans to publish addition
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed to pursuing legislative reform for micromobility vehicles when parliamentary time allows by creating a ‘Low-speed Zero Emission Vehicle’ category. This could be used to regulate pavement robots in future. Any new regulations w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Tests: Edinburgh
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the answer to Question UIN 63826 on driving test waiting times in Currie, what the average waiting time is for a practical driving test at the Currie Driving Test Centre; and by what date she
writtenQuestions.answerText: The average waiting time for a car practical driving test at Currie driving test centre in May was 24 weeks.DVSA is taking sustained action to increase testing capacity and reduce waiting times as quickly as possible, including recruiting and training mor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Pancreatic Cancer: Genomics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the barriers to genomic testing for pancreatic cancer patients and how he plans to increase uptake nationally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Genomic testing in the National Health Service in England is provided through the NHS Genomic Medicine Service (NHS GMS) and delivered by a national genomic testing network of seven NHS Genomic Laboratory Hubs (GLHs). The NHS GLHs deliver testing as direc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Genomics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he will undertake a horizon-scanning review to identify emerging actionable gene mutations for timely inclusion in the National Genomic Test Directory, prioritising pancreatic cancer and ot
writtenQuestions.answerText: Genomic testing in the National Health Service in England is provided through the NHS Genomic Medicine Service and delivered by a national genomic testing network of seven NHS Genomic Laboratory Hubs (GLHs). The NHS GLHs deliver testing as directed by the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Centre for the Validation of Alternative Methods: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of funding to the UK Centre for the Validation of Alternative Methods; what assessment he has made of the role of industry co-investment in s
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is supporting the establishment of the UK Centre for the Validation of Alternative Methods through funding secured as part of the Replacing Animals in Science strategy, with costs informed by analysis of validation requirements and comparab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Driverless Vehicles: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether the Centre for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles is exploring the adequacy of compensation arrangements for victims of accidents involving uninsured vehicles in the context of the Automated Vehicles Act
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the Automated Vehicles Act Implementation Programme, officials from the Centre for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles are working with the Motor Insurers' Bureau on insurance for automated vehicles. This includes assessing the adequacy of the insur...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Micromobility Vehicles: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to launch the consultation on micromobility regulation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government plans to consult on e-scooter regulations over the next year.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to help tackle harmful platform features that put children at risk online.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting children online is a priority for the Secretary of State. Through our recent consultation, which included parents and children's consultations and nationally representative polls, we heard from over 116,000 people. Following this, on 15 June th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she plans to extend proposed restrictions to livestreaming and communication with strangers for child users to other high-risk functionalities including disappearing messages, t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recently announced restrictions on social media services offering their services to under-16s and wider services offering livestreaming or stranger communication to under-16s.Combined with requiring more device level protections for childre...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: Environment Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of permanent absolute GDP growth on long-term environmental sustainability in the context of the Dasgupta Review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the vital role of nature in the economy and agrees with the central conclusion of the Dasgupta Review that biodiversity underpins nature, and sustains our economies, livelihoods and well-being. As recommended by the Dasgupta Revi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: Nature Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps her Department is taking to (a) cost the degradation of natural assets resulting from headline GDP growth and (b) ensure long-term environmental costs are reflected in fiscal planning.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the Dasgupta Review, the Government has taken forward work to integrate nature into policy-making, including improving the valuation of natural capital through collaboration with Defra and its Biodiversity Expert Committee. The Government is support...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Human Rights: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to promote and support the adoption of the (a) Rights Respecting Schools programme and (b) principles of the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child in schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to upholding the principles of the UN Convention of the Rights of the Child and safeguarding the rights of children within schools. For example, the Children’s Wellbeing and Schools Act included several measures to enhance chil...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Payroll Deduction Scheme: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what guidance HM Revenue and Customs has issued to employers on the legality and tax treatment of schemes known as grocery salary sacrifice schemes; what steps have they taken to ensure such schemes are structured t
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) publishes guidance on the tax treatment of salary sacrifice arrangements, including the optional remuneration arrangements rules in the Income Tax (Earnings and Pensions) Act 2003. Where arrangements are marketed as providing...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Novel Foods: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what discussions his Department has had with the Food Standards Agency on the regulatory pathway for novel foods.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers and officials regularly discuss novel food regulatory pathways with FSA. This includes the Engineering Biology Sandbox and Regulatory Innovation Office’s financial support for a new FSA innovation hub.In October 2024, DSIT awarded the Food Stand...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the change in funding levels for the Independent Commission for Aid Impact on the evaluation of the (a) effectiveness
writtenQuestions.answerText: The reduction in the Independent Commission for Aid Impact's (ICAI's) budget is proportionate in the context of the reduction of Official Development Assistance (ODA) to 0.3 per cent of Gross National Income, and provides sufficient funding to maintain it...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Meat and Proteins: Novel Foods
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential merits of incorporation of cultivated meat and alternative proteins into future food security and agri-tech strategies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra is researching the sector’s growth potential, impacts on future emissions, and working across government to support innovation and scaling. Defra is also working with the Food Standards Agency to develop guidance for approval of new alternative prot...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
